AccountOption.description: A cross-account switch target for the system-admin account picker.
AccountOption.title: AccountOption

AccountRef.description:
An account the identity belongs to — the active one or a switch target.

Carries ``status`` (e.g. active | suspended) so the UI can flag disabled/suspended accounts.
AccountRef.title: AccountRef

ActivePrincipal.description: The principal (human / agent / service_account) the user is acting as this session.
ActivePrincipal.title: ActivePrincipal

AdminAccountView.description: An account as the admin console sees it — the full row, not the trimmed switch-target shape.
AdminAccountView.title: AdminAccountView

AgentGenerationInfo.description: A persisted agent-definition generation, recorded when a new fingerprint is first seen.
AgentGenerationInfo.title: AgentGenerationInfo

AgentQualityRollup.description:
Per-agent quality over a window. `avg_score` is the headline human rating,
combining direct agent scores and the human scores on that agent's runs
(PRD: agent views combine direct + run scores). `graded_runs` counts the
distinct runs that carry a human quality score; `direct_count` the offline
grades on the agent itself. `avg_llm_score`/`llm_graded_runs` are the parallel
auto-grade (H1) signal — kept separate so the LLM judge never moves the human
numbers; null/0 when the agent has no auto-grades in the window.
AgentQualityRollup.title: AgentQualityRollup

ApiKeyBoundTo.description:
What kind of principal an API key acts as. Derived from the bound principal's kind, not a
stored column — a key on a ``human`` principal is user-bound, otherwise service-account.
ApiKeyBoundTo.title: ApiKeyBoundTo

AuditSortKey.description:
Allow-listed columns the Audit page may sort by. Only these map to a real (indexed-friendly)
ordering expression — an arbitrary client string can never reach the ``ORDER BY``, so this is
also the injection guard for server-side sort.
AuditSortKey.title: AuditSortKey

AuthSource.description: What the IdP / auth method asserts about this session — for display/debug, not authz.
AuthSource.title: AuthSource

AuthUser.description: The identity-spine user — the global login identity.
AuthUser.title: AuthUser

AuthzContext.description:
The caller's authorization view — a "display extra" (like ``is_system_admin``) hydrated only on
the ``/auth/me`` path, for nav-gating admin affordances. The server still enforces every action
per-resource; this is UX only.

``capabilities`` is the coarse, deduped set of capability keys the acting principal holds anywhere
in the active account (no resource scope). ``mode`` is this caller's resolved ``EnforcementMode``
string value ``"off"``/``"shadow"``/``"enforce"`` (typed as ``str`` to keep the identity module
free of an authz import, which would cycle): the UI must gate nav **only** when
``mode == "enforce"`` — under off/shadow the server allows everything, so hiding tabs would be
wrong (see #953).
AuthzContext.title: AuthzContext

AuthzMember.description: A member row for the list / attribute-edit views — adds the user-managed ABAC attributes.
AuthzMember.title: AuthzMember

AuthzServiceAccount.description: A service-account row for the list / attribute-edit views — adds ABAC attributes.
AuthzServiceAccount.title: AuthzServiceAccount

AuthzServiceAccountRoles.description: A service account's role-assignment result (what ``PUT …/service-accounts/{id}/roles`` returns).
AuthzServiceAccountRoles.title: AuthzServiceAccountRoles

AutoScoreSeries.description:
Ordered per-run auto-grade (LLM) quality values for one agent over a window,
oldest first. The Quality Lab saturation view computes a running mean ± SEM from
this flat series client-side to show whether the agent's average auto-score has
stabilized as auto-evals accumulated.
AutoScoreSeries.title: AutoScoreSeries

CatalogTool.description:
One upstream tool in the persisted catalog snapshot.

``name`` is the gateway-exposed namespaced name (``"<namespace>_<bare>"``);
``bare_name`` is the unprefixed name on the upstream. Carries enough schema to
reconstruct the MCP wire form without re-querying upstreams.

bare_name.description: Unprefixed tool name on the upstream server.
bare_name.title: Bare Name

name.description: Namespaced tool name as exposed by the gateway.
name.title: Name

namespace.description: Owning server's name; the mount namespace.
namespace.title: Namespace

parameters.description: JSON schema for the tool's input.
parameters.title: Parameters

CatalogTool.title: CatalogTool

CreateAgentDraftRequest.description:
Create (or resume) a draft agent from the Add-Agent wizard's first step.

``agent_id`` is the operator's friendly per-account name (the id_slug); the control plane freezes
an opaque internal agent_id against it at draft-create time and returns it. ``config`` is the
opaque wizard state, persisted for cross-device resume.
CreateAgentDraftRequest.title: CreateAgentDraftRequest

CreateServerRequest.description: ``ServerCreate`` + optional ABAC ``labels`` for the control-plane register API.

allow.description: Glob patterns of tool names to expose (empty = all).
allow.title: Allow

auth.description: Upstream auth scheme. 'oauth' runs FastMCP's browser OAuth flow on first connect and caches tokens persistently.

deny.description: Glob patterns of tool names to hide (wins over allow).
deny.title: Deny

name.description: Unique name; also used as the mount namespace/prefix. Letters, digits and hyphens only — no underscore (it is the namespace/tool-name separator).
name.title: Name

oauth_client_id.description: OAuth client id for the client_credentials grant (not secret).
oauth_client_id.title: Oauth Client Id

oauth_client_secret.description: OAuth client secret as a ${env:VAR} or ${file:path} reference, resolved at connect time. Raw secrets are rejected so the registry never stores one.
oauth_client_secret.title: Oauth Client Secret

oauth_scopes.description: OAuth scopes to request; empty = server/registration default.
oauth_scopes.title: Oauth Scopes

oauth_token_url.description: Token endpoint for the client_credentials grant.
oauth_token_url.title: Oauth Token Url

static_headers.description: Headers always sent upstream; merged under hook-provided headers. Values may embed ${env:VAR} / ${file:path} secret references resolved at connect time — use those for credentials (e.g. 'Bearer ${env:API_TOKEN}'), because this field is stored in plaintext and returned by the admin read API.
static_headers.title: Static Headers

url.description: Endpoint URL for the upstream MCP server.
url.title: Url

CreateServerRequest.title: CreateServerRequest

CredentialBinding.description:
An agent allowed to use a credential (push-delivery access policy).

``on_demand`` additionally lets a worker authenticated as this agent pull the
value outside of a run (e.g. a boot-time token); per-run push is unaffected.
CredentialBinding.title: CredentialBinding

CredentialMetadata.description:
Public, value-free view of a stored credential.

The encrypted value is never part of this model: it is write-only after
creation and only the Control Plane decrypt boundary ever sees plaintext.
CredentialMetadata.title: CredentialMetadata

EndpointWebhookAcceptedResponse.description:
Response for the standalone endpoint ingest route (``/webhooks/endpoint/{trigger_id}``).

A standalone endpoint accepts and captures the payload regardless of whether a target is
attached. ``routed`` is true when it dispatched to an agent/workflow; when no target is wired
yet it is false (the payload is captured for inspection but nothing runs).
EndpointWebhookAcceptedResponse.title: EndpointWebhookAcceptedResponse

GradeContext.description:
The run under evaluation, pulled by a grader from the control plane.

Judging is on outcomes — the run's input and output — not the execution path
(Anthropic's anti-brittleness rule). ``guidelines`` is the per-binding free-text
rubric (H1.3) resolved from the grader binding at grade time; when absent the
grader applies a generic rubric. ``run_id`` is the *target* run being graded.
GradeContext.title: GradeContext

allow.description: Group-level allow globs, applied on top of each member server's rules (empty = inherit server). Can only narrow: a tool the owning server denies stays denied even if a group allow matches it.
allow.title: Allow

deny.description: Group-level deny globs, layered on top of server rules; deny wins.
deny.title: Deny

name.description: Unique group name; used in the /mcp/g/{group} route and as the policy key.
name.title: Name

GroupPatch.description: Partial update for a group; unset fields are left unchanged.
GroupPatch.title: GroupPatch

GroupRecord.description: A persisted group, as returned by the store and admin API.

allow.description: Group-level allow globs, applied on top of each member server's rules (empty = inherit server). Can only narrow: a tool the owning server denies stays denied even if a group allow matches it.
allow.title: Allow

deny.description: Group-level deny globs, layered on top of server rules; deny wins.
deny.title: Deny

name.description: Unique group name; used in the /mcp/g/{group} route and as the policy key.
name.title: Name

GroupRecord.title: GroupRecord

IdentityContext.description: The resolved identity for a session: who they are, how they signed in, and where.
IdentityContext.title: IdentityContext

ImpersonationContext.description:
Set only while a session is acting under a system-admin impersonation or cross-account view.

Its presence *is* the "you are impersonating" signal; it names the originating admin so the UI
can show a banner with an Exit affordance and audit can attribute the real actor (the ``user``/
``account``/``principal`` on the enclosing :class:`IdentityContext` describe the TARGET).
ImpersonationContext.title: ImpersonationContext

ImpersonationMode.description:
How a system-admin session is acting. Stored in the signed cookie's ``act_as`` claim and
surfaced on :class:`ImpersonationContext`.
ImpersonationMode.title: ImpersonationMode

IncidentDetailResponse.description:
``GET /incidents/{id}`` response — the incident detail plus its aggregate investigation stats
(duration/tokens/cost/errors), flattened onto the same object the UI's ``PlatformIncidentDetail``
already expects (additive field, so it's wire-compatible before the UI's stats card ships).
IncidentDetailResponse.title: IncidentDetailResponse

IntegrationConnectionDetail.description: Connection metadata. Secret values are never returned.
IntegrationConnectionDetail.title: IntegrationConnectionDetail

LabeledServerRecord.description:
Control-plane view of a server: the tenant-agnostic ``fast_gateway.ServerRecord`` plus this
product's ABAC ``labels`` (#884 Phase D). The labels live only in the control-plane ``mcp_servers``
table, so the reusable gateway library/model stays label-free; this subclass surfaces them on the
admin read API without changing the library contract.

allow.description: Glob patterns of tool names to expose (empty = all).
allow.title: Allow

auth.description: Upstream auth scheme. 'oauth' runs FastMCP's browser OAuth flow on first connect and caches tokens persistently.

deny.description: Glob patterns of tool names to hide (wins over allow).
deny.title: Deny

name.description: Unique name; also used as the mount namespace/prefix. Letters, digits and hyphens only — no underscore (it is the namespace/tool-name separator).
name.title: Name

oauth_client_id.description: OAuth client id for the client_credentials grant (not secret).
oauth_client_id.title: Oauth Client Id

oauth_client_secret.description: OAuth client secret as a ${env:VAR} or ${file:path} reference, resolved at connect time. Raw secrets are rejected so the registry never stores one.
oauth_client_secret.title: Oauth Client Secret

oauth_scopes.description: OAuth scopes to request; empty = server/registration default.
oauth_scopes.title: Oauth Scopes

oauth_token_url.description: Token endpoint for the client_credentials grant.
oauth_token_url.title: Oauth Token Url

static_headers.description: Headers always sent upstream; merged under hook-provided headers. Values may embed ${env:VAR} / ${file:path} secret references resolved at connect time — use those for credentials (e.g. 'Bearer ${env:API_TOKEN}'), because this field is stored in plaintext and returned by the admin read API.
static_headers.title: Static Headers

url.description: Endpoint URL for the upstream MCP server.
url.title: Url

LabeledServerRecord.title: LabeledServerRecord

ManualSetupInstructions.description:
Copy-paste fallback for a repo whose hook could not be auto-created (missing App
permission, PAT integration, or no GitHub integration connected at all).
ManualSetupInstructions.title: ManualSetupInstructions

PolicyAction.description: Actions a policy rule can prescribe.
PolicyAction.title: PolicyAction

PolicyCondition.description: A single condition evaluated against execution context.
PolicyCondition.title: PolicyCondition

PolicyDefaults.description:
Default settings applied when no rule matches.

The first four fields are language/runtime budgets evaluated by the
rule engine. The remaining fields are **sandbox resource constraints**
consumed by sandbox providers (Azure, Docker, Hyperlight) and are
ignored by the rule engine itself.

max_cpu.description: Sandbox CPU limit in vCPUs (e.g. 0.5, 1.0). None = provider default.
max_cpu.title: Max Cpu

max_memory_mb.description: Sandbox memory limit in MiB. None = provider default.
max_memory_mb.title: Max Memory Mb

network_default.description: Default sandbox egress action when a host is not on network_allowlist. 'deny' is fail-closed and is the default. Set to 'allow' only for trusted dev/research workloads.
network_default.title: Network Default

timeout_seconds.description: Per-execute_code wall-clock cap. None = provider default.
timeout_seconds.title: Timeout Seconds

PolicyDefaults.title: PolicyDefaults

inherit.description: If false, parent governance.yaml files are not loaded (stops inheritance)
inherit.title: Inherit

network_allowlist.description: Host patterns the sandbox may reach (e.g. 'pypi.org', '*.github.com'). Combined with defaults.network_default to form the sandbox egress policy.
network_allowlist.title: Network Allowlist

scope.description: Glob pattern — policy only applies when action path matches
scope.title: Scope

tool_allowlist.description: Tool names the agent may invoke. Enforced host-side by the PolicyEvaluator before any sandbox call.
tool_allowlist.title: Tool Allowlist

PolicyGrantInput.description:
A subjectless member grant a policy bundles: a single capability with its scope. No subject/role
(that is a binding grant) and no id/effect on input (effect is allow-only for v1).
PolicyGrantInput.title: PolicyGrantInput

PolicyRule.description: A single governance rule within a policy document.

override.description: If true, replaces a parent rule with the same name during folder-level merge
override.title: Override

priority.description: Higher priority rules are evaluated first
priority.title: Priority

PolicyRule.title: PolicyRule

RunEventIn.description: Unified event sent from SDK → controlplane via the ingest endpoint.
RunEventIn.title: RunEventIn

RunQualityRow.description:
A run paired with its human quality score, for the per-agent
sort/filter-by-score list ("worst runs and why" in one click). `avg_score` is
null for an ungraded run; `rationale`/`reviewer`/`scored_at` come from the
most recent score on the run.
RunQualityRow.title: RunQualityRow

RunUsage.description: Token and cost accounting for a run, or for a single ``token.created`` event.
RunUsage.title: RunUsage

ScoreValue.description:
A score's value, typed. `numeric` is the canonical **0..1 float** (H1.1
re-base) for both `numeric` ratings (human 1-5 stars map (n-1)/4 → 0..1; the LLM
judge writes a continuous 0..1) and `boolean` (0 or 1); `label` holds a short
categorical value. 0..1 is the one scale that fits 1-5, 0-100%, and thumbs alike.
ScoreValue.title: ScoreValue

ServerAuth.description:
Upstream authentication scheme used when connecting to a registered server.

``oauth`` is the interactive browser authorization-code flow (Mode B only);
``oauth_client_credentials`` is the headless machine-to-machine grant. Static
token / API-key auth needs no scheme: put a ``${env:}``/``${file:}`` secret
reference in ``static_headers`` and it resolves at connect time.
ServerAuth.title: ServerAuth

SigningCredentialRef.description: Reference to the credential a webhook validates against (never the secret value).
SigningCredentialRef.title: SigningCredentialRef

SkillQualityRollup.description:
Per-skill quality over a window. `avg_score` pools two honest signals (M5):
DIRECT offline skill grades and the human grades on runs that USED the skill
(correlational — "runs using this skill rate X", not a causal claim). A run is
attributed to a skill via its `agentops.skill.invoke` telemetry, matched on
skill_id; unknown skill usage is excluded, never guessed. `attributed_runs`
counts the distinct runs that contributed; `direct_count` the offline grades.
`avg_llm_score`/`llm_attributed_runs` are the parallel auto-grade (H1) signal,
kept separate from the human numbers; null/0 when there are no auto-grades.
SkillQualityRollup.title: SkillQualityRollup

Transport.description:
Transport used to reach an upstream MCP server. The gateway proxies remote
servers only - local stdio subprocesses are out of scope.
Transport.title: Transport

TrustedDomainSettingsView.description:
The Account-settings view of trusted domains for the current account and caller.

``domains`` lists every trusted domain on the account (including any an operator registered), so
the owner can see the full picture. ``eligible_domain`` is the caller's own self-serviceable
domain — non-null only when their sign-in email is an eligible (non-consumer) domain; the UI
renders the auto-join toggle for it. ``ineligible_reason`` explains a null ``eligible_domain``
(consumer email / no domain) so the UI can show why the toggle is unavailable. ``enabled`` is
whether ``eligible_domain`` is currently trusted (the toggle's state).
TrustedDomainSettingsView.title: TrustedDomainSettingsView

UngradedQueueResponse.description:
The grading queue: the true backlog `total` of finished, ungraded runs plus
the first `runs` of them (capped by the endpoint's `limit`). `total` is what the
"ungraded runs" headline counts — it must not be confused with `len(runs)`.
UngradedQueueResponse.title: UngradedQueueResponse

UpdateAgentDraftRequest.description:
Autosave the wizard state for an existing draft (draft-only; a promoted agent rejects this).

``config`` is optional: ``None`` (omitted) leaves the stored config untouched, so a display-name-only
patch never wipes the saved wizard state. The wizard always sends the full config.
UpdateAgentDraftRequest.title: UpdateAgentDraftRequest

UpdateCredentialMetadataRequest.description: Metadata-only patch. The value cannot be changed here — use replace.
UpdateCredentialMetadataRequest.title: UpdateCredentialMetadataRequest

UpdateServerRequest.description: ``ServerPatch`` + ABAC ``labels`` (None → leave unchanged; a dict incl. ``{}`` REPLACES).
UpdateServerRequest.title: UpdateServerRequest

WebhookSample.description:
A captured inbound webhook payload sample (debugging aid). Never carries the auth
token/signature header — those are stripped before storage. ``body`` is the parsed JSON
payload, size-capped; when the raw body exceeded the cap, ``truncated`` is true and ``body``
holds a truncated string preview instead of the parsed object.
WebhookSample.title: WebhookSample

WebhookSampleStatus.description: Disposition recorded for a captured inbound webhook sample.
WebhookSampleStatus.title: WebhookSampleStatus

WorkerCatalogCredential.description: A credential the catalog worker needs the account to have configured.
WorkerCatalogCredential.title: WorkerCatalogCredential

WorkerCatalogDeployRequest.description:
Deploy a catalog worker. The server pins the image and derives the customer/credentials —
the client only names the instance.
WorkerCatalogDeployRequest.title: WorkerCatalogDeployRequest

WorkerCatalogEntryResponse.description: A curated worker the user can deploy, plus this account's readiness to deploy it.
WorkerCatalogEntryResponse.title: WorkerCatalogEntryResponse

WorkerCatalogIntegration.description:
An integration connection the catalog worker needs bound at deploy time, in lieu of a plain
secret credential pair (e.g. AWS) — see ``worker_catalog.catalog.CatalogIntegration``.
WorkerCatalogIntegration.title: WorkerCatalogIntegration

WorkerCatalogSelfHostedDeployRequest.description:
Self-hosted deploy of a catalog worker. The server validates credentials/integrations,
mints a worker token, and returns install instructions with the catalog image.
WorkerCatalogSelfHostedDeployRequest.title: WorkerCatalogSelfHostedDeployRequest

WorkerCatalogSelfHostedDeployResponse.description: Response from a self-hosted catalog deploy: the token (shown once) and the agent id.
WorkerCatalogSelfHostedDeployResponse.title: WorkerCatalogSelfHostedDeployResponse

WorkerInstanceInfo.description:
One live (or recently-seen) worker replica of a logical agent.

``busy`` means the worker currently owns a run in ``claimed`` or ``running`` (the queue branch
enforces at most one active run per worker). ``is_online`` reflects heartbeat freshness. A
``free`` instance (in the aggregate counts on :class:`AgentInstanceResponse`) is one that is
online but not busy.
WorkerInstanceInfo.title: WorkerInstanceInfo

WorkerSecretsResponse.description:
The on-demand credentials a worker may pull for itself, ``{name: value}``.

Returned only for the authenticated agent's bindings flagged ``on_demand``.
Plaintext crosses the wire here; the worker must not log or persist it.
WorkerSecretsResponse.title: WorkerSecretsResponse

WorkerTokenStatusResponse.description:
Read-only lifecycle status of an agent's worker token. Carries NO secret material — the token
plaintext is shown only once, on create/rotate. Powers the "view + revoke" UI surface.
WorkerTokenStatusResponse.title: WorkerTokenStatusResponse

WorkerTokenSummaryResponse.description:
One worker token in an account, for the Settings token table. Carries NO secret material.

Sourced from the ``worker-token-<agent_id>`` credentials directly (not from agent_instances), so
tokens of not-yet-installed agents — minted but never heart-beaten, hence with no agent row —
still appear. Agent metadata (``id_slug``/``name``/``is_archived``/``agent_status``) is null-ish
for those orphan tokens: only the opaque ``agent_id`` is known until the worker registers.
WorkerTokenSummaryResponse.title: WorkerTokenSummaryResponse

post.description:
Cancel an in-flight A2A task (== run): flip it to cancelled and signal the worker to interrupt.

Account-scoped like every read here, so a cross-account task id reads as not-found. Already-terminal
tasks return their current state (idempotent) rather than erroring the A2A client.

schema.title: Response Service-Accounts List Service Accounts Endpoint
Response Service-Accounts Disable Service Account Endpoint

delete.description:
Disable SSO auto-join for the caller's own email domain. As with enable, the domain is derived
from the caller's identity, so self-serve can only remove the caller's own domain (operators use
the admin console to remove any domain). 404 if that domain isn't trusted on the account.

get.description: The current account's trusted domains plus the caller's self-serve eligibility (toggle state).

post.description:
Enable SSO auto-join for the caller's own email domain. The domain is derived server-side from
the caller's identity — never taken from the request — so an owner can only ever trust their own
domain. 422 if that domain isn't eligible (consumer email / none); 409 if already trusted.

get.description: All accounts, optionally name/slug-filtered (newest first). Read-only — not audited.

post.description:
Create a new account. The insert + its audit row commit together; a duplicate slug rolls the
whole thing back and returns 409.

patch.description:
Rename and/or edit an account's free-form ``settings``. ``slug`` and ``status`` are not
editable here (status flips go through suspend/reactivate).

post.description: Reactivate a suspended account.
Suspend an account (walls the tenant off; no hard delete).

get.description: List an account's trusted domains. Read-only — not audited.

post.description:
Register a trusted domain. 409 if this account already has the domain (the same domain may
still be registered on other accounts); 422 if it isn't a valid email domain.

delete.description:
Unregister a trusted domain from the account. 404 if the account or domain isn't found.
``reason`` is a required query param (DELETE bodies are unreliable across proxies).

get.description: Activity + inventory counts for one account (404 if the account doesn't exist).
Cross-account totals + the busiest accounts by recent runs.
Cross-account platform-admin activity feed (newest first). Optionally scope to one account.

Raw authz decisions over the window, newest first. ``deny_only`` is the deny drill-down (keyed
on the PDP verdict, so shadow would-denies are included); ``account`` (a slug)/``action`` narrow
further.

parameters.description: account slug; omit for all accounts
parameters.name: account

schema.description: account slug; omit for all accounts
schema.title: Account

get.description:
Per-action authz-decision roll-up over the window, busiest first. Cross-account unless
``account`` (a slug) scopes it to one tenant.

parameters.description: account slug; omit for all accounts
parameters.name: account

schema.description: account slug; omit for all accounts
schema.title: Account

get.description:
Registered agents (offline first) with heartbeat + reachability. Scope to one account with
``account_id`` — the console requires picking one; an unscoped call spans all accounts.
Currently-active impersonation / account-view sessions, newest first. Read-only — not audited.

post.description:
Kill a session: add its ``sid`` to the revocation denylist so the next request on it is logged
out (cluster-wide). 404 if the session isn't currently active (already stopped/revoked/expired).

get.description:
Read-only roster of platform admins. Grants/revokes are CLI/break-glass only — there is no
mutation here by design.

post.description:
Mint a revocable worker token and render a self-deploy ``helm install`` command + values.

Operator-only (``require_user``): creating a worker token is an operator action; a worker token must not reach it.

Create (or resume) a *draft* agent for the Add-Agent wizard's first step.

Freezes an opaque agent_id against the friendly name so the wizard is resumable by id and the
eventual worker token binds to it. Idempotent per name: an in-progress draft is returned as-is
(the caller routes into resume), a live agent's name yields 409. Operator-only (``require_user``).

Create (or rotate) a revocable worker token for a friendly agent id, without a full self-deploy render.

Operator-only (``require_user``): a worker token must never create another worker token, regardless of authz mode.

get.description:
List every worker token in the account for the Settings token table (metadata only).

Sourced from the worker-token credentials, so tokens of not-yet-installed agents (minted but never
heart-beaten, hence with no agent row) appear alongside registered/draft ones. Declared before the
``/{agent_id}`` routes so the literal path wins. authz_filter narrows to the agents the caller may
read (§5b), AND-ed onto the account scope inside the service; allow-all while authz is disabled.

schema.title: Response Agents List Worker Tokens Route

patch.description: Disable an agent so its triggers stop firing. Manual invocations still work.
Autosave the wizard config for a draft agent. Draft-only (a live agent returns 409). Operator-only.
Enable an agent so its triggers may fire. Disabled agents already accept manual invocations.

delete.description: Revoke the self-deployed agent's worker token. Idempotent. Operator-only (``require_user``).

get.description:
Read the agent's worker-token lifecycle status (exists / created / rotated / expiry).

Metadata only — never the token value. Backs the per-agent "Worker token" panel so an operator can
see whether a token is live before rotating or revoking it. ``agent_id`` is the friendly handle or
the opaque id; ``resolve_agent_id`` maps either to the canonical opaque id (and falls back to the
raw ref when no agent row exists yet — a self-registering agent's token may already be minted,
keyed by that id, so we must not 404 a token that is real).

schema.title: Response Api-Keys List Api Keys Endpoint
Response Api-Keys Revoke Api Key Endpoint

parameters.description: Filter to one platform area, e.g. 'authorization'.
parameters.name: category

schema.description: Filter to one platform area, e.g. 'authorization'.
schema.title: Category

parameters.description: Free-text match on action, operation, and actor (name/email/id).
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Free-text match on action, operation, and actor (name/email/id).
schema.title: Q
Response Credentials List Credentials For Agent

get.description: Return aggregated upstream tools, optionally filtered by a search query.

post.description: Connect to the upstream and perform the MCP handshake.

get.description: Live introspection of an upstream's tools.

delete.description:
Clean up a cloud deploy the user is abandoning (in-progress or failed), keyed by
``runtime_agent_id`` — distinct from ``delete_hosted_agent`` above, which decommissions a
live GitOps-tracked agent by ``(customer, agent_id)``. Never gates on ``deploy_status``: the
user may cancel an in-progress deploy just as well as clean up a failed one.

schema.title: Response Incident-Pipelines List Incident Pipelines Endpoint

get.description:
Resolve a channel name to an ID.

Tries users_conversations first (fast — bot-member channels only).
Falls back to conversations_list with the user token for channels the
bot hasn't joined yet.

409.description: A knowledge base with this name already exists
A knowledge base with this name already exists

post.description:
Ingest one or more documents. Metadata form fields apply to every file in the batch;
`title` only applies to a single-file upload (multi-file titles come from each file's
OKF frontmatter or filename). The count cap is validated before any body is read (so
peak buffering is bounded at MAX_FILES_PER_BATCH x MAX_DOCUMENT_BYTES); per-file size
validation runs after buffering but before any ingest, so an oversized batch or file
rejects the whole request. A mid-batch ingest failure leaves the already-ingested files
in place (same outcome as uploading them one by one).

schema.title: Response Review-Workflows List Review Workflows Endpoint

get.description:
Repos the account's connected GitHub App integration can see, for the wizard's repo
picker. Empty (not an error) when no App-based integration is connected — the wizard falls
back to manual repo entry + manual webhook instructions in that case.

schema.title: Response Review-Workflows List Github Repos Endpoint

get.description:
Unguarded, like the other Code Review Platform list endpoints — this is a picker/catalog
read, not a sensitive resource, and reuses the account's existing agent-read visibility.

Latest `limit` runs newest-first (optionally scoped to one ``agent_id``), or — with
?thread_id — the full continuation chain for one thread, oldest-first.

post.description:
Cancel an active run (queued/claimed/running → cancelled).

Queued cancel is exact (the run just leaves the queue). For a claimed/running run the owning worker
is also signalled to interrupt the in-flight handler (gated by ``run_cancel_signal_enabled``); if
that can't be delivered the cancel falls back to advisory — the worker's later report is a no-op and
its result is discarded.

Create a new run for the same agent, seeded with the prior session snapshot.

Restoring the snapshot alone gives the agent nothing to act on; a revived run
still drives a turn with input. With no body input we reuse the original
run's input (resume a pending/failed turn); with body input we use it as the
next turn (continue a completed run).

Delivery mirrors ``/invoke``: prefer the reverse SSE channel (the only way to
reach outbound-only workers — staging/prod live in a separate namespace the CP
cannot dial), and fall back to a direct A2A POST only when no channel is
connected (local/dev). The snapshot rides either path as wire-only metadata.

get.description:
Active accounts (optionally name/slug-filtered) — the system-admin cross-account switch
targets. System-admin only; ordinary members switch among ``member_accounts`` from /auth/me
instead. Only ACTIVE accounts are returned (you can't switch into a suspended tenant), and
``limit`` is unbounded by default so the switcher pulls every active account once and resolves
any slug client-side — no silent miss for an account past a fixed cap. A positive ``limit`` still
caps the result if a caller wants to page.

post.description:
Mint a session that acts AS the target human in their account. System-admin only. The target
becomes the effective identity (acting with their own permissions); the admin is preserved in the
``impersonator_*`` claims and the action is audited.

Revert an impersonation / account-view session back to the originating admin's own session.

Authorized by the signed cookie itself: only a session that carries ``impersonator_*`` claims can
call this, and it restores exactly that admin — no system-admin re-check (the effective user is
the impersonated target, which is deliberately NOT treated as admin).

Switch the active account. If the caller is a member of the target, switch to their own
principal there (ordinary org switch). Otherwise it requires a system admin, who enters via the
account's hidden ``system`` principal (cross-account view; writes flag-gated by the middleware).

schema.title: Response Triggers List Trigger Samples Endpoint

post.description: Standalone endpoint ingest — stable id-based URL, independent of any target.

get.description:
Return the run a grader was dispatched to evaluate, plus its binding's guidelines.

Worker-authenticated and authorized by grade-run ownership (when worker auth is on, the
authenticated caller must own ``grade_run_id``), so a grader pulls only the run it was asked
to grade — it never reads arbitrary runs and never writes scores. Lets the control plane push
an empty grade-run payload instead of inlining (and persisting a duplicate of) the target
run's I/O. With worker auth off (open/local clusters) the caller is unauthenticated and the
grader is taken from the grade run itself.
Return a short-lived GitHub App installation token for authenticated workers.

schema.title: Response Workers Fetch Github Integration Token

get.description:
Pull the on-demand credentials bound to the calling worker's agent.

The agent identity is the one the worker authenticated as (``X-Agent-Id``),
never a client-supplied parameter, so a worker can only fetch its own secrets.

409.description: A workflow with this name already exists
A workflow with this name already exists

schema.title: Response Workflows List Workflow Runs Endpoint
Response Workflows List Workflow Run Steps Endpoint

get.description:
Serve the dev/preview login helper page (see ``_DEV_LOGIN_HTML``).

Gated by the same toggle as the exchange itself. Open it from the preview at
``http://localhost:5173/auth/dev/login`` (vite proxies ``/auth`` to the control plane).

Clear the local session and, for OIDC logins, end the IdP session too.

RP-initiated logout: redirect to the provider's end_session_endpoint with
post_logout_redirect_uri (registered on the app) so the IdP ends its session and bounces
back. Without ending the IdP session, the still-active SSO session would silently log the
user back in.

NOTE: we no longer carry the id_token in the session cookie (see the OIDC callback hotfix
re: the 4096-byte cookie limit), so no id_token_hint is sent. Auth0 still ends the session
and redirects via client_id + a registered post_logout_redirect_uri. Restore the hint once
a server-side session store lands and the id_token is available again.

post.description:
Clear the local session and, for OIDC logins, end the IdP session too.

RP-initiated logout: redirect to the provider's end_session_endpoint with
post_logout_redirect_uri (registered on the app) so the IdP ends its session and bounces
back. Without ending the IdP session, the still-active SSO session would silently log the
user back in.

NOTE: we no longer carry the id_token in the session cookie (see the OIDC callback hotfix
re: the 4096-byte cookie limit), so no id_token_hint is sent. Auth0 still ends the session
and redirects via client_id + a registered post_logout_redirect_uri. Restore the hint once
a server-side session store lands and the id_token is available again.

get.description:
Begin the OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE flow.

With ``silent=1`` the authorize request carries ``prompt=none``: the IdP reuses an existing
SSO session and redirects straight back with a code (no login UI), or — when no live SSO
session exists — returns ``error=login_required`` to the callback, which then falls back to an
interactive login. This is how a control-plane session (a hard-capped signed cookie, no refresh
token) is transparently renewed against the longer-lived IdP session.

post.description:
Exchange a human-bound API key for the same signed session cookie the OIDC callback issues.

This is the non-interactive login path: a key minted with ``apikey mint --user-email`` acts as a
human's per-account principal, so we mint that user's session directly — no Auth0 browser
redirect. It's how the localhost-only Claude Code preview (and automated UI tests) log in as a
real user. No privilege escalation: the bearer already grants full API access as that principal;
this just additionally enables the browser/UI surface for the *same* identity.

Requires ``Authorization: Bearer <api-key>``. The key must be bound to a ``human`` principal —
a plain ``service_account`` key has no user to represent and is rejected (400).

get.description:
Login entry point.

The SPA's RequireAuth redirects here when the session is missing/expired, so the client
doesn't need to construct the provider login URL itself: this 302s to a *silent* OIDC login
(``/auth/oidc/login?silent=1``) via ``login_path``, preserving ``next``. A live IdP SSO session
is then renewed with no UI; if there is none, the callback falls back to an interactive login.
When auth is disabled (local dev) there is nothing to log into → bounce home.
